name: Extract structured data from server logs
prompt: Parse server.log and extract structured data from each line. Each log line contains a timestamp, log level, IP address, HTTP method, path, status code, and response time. Some lines also have an error message in quotes. Some paths include query strings (e.g. /edge/search?tag=video) — extract just the path without the query string. Write the results to extracted.json as an array of objects with fields: timestamp, level, ip, method, path (without query string), status (number), duration_ms (number), and message (string, only if present). The message field should only exist on ERROR entries.

criteria.testPattern: is an array|correct number|required fields|status is a number|duration_ms is a number

criteria.description: Correct IP and log level counts
criteria.testPattern: unique IPs|ERROR entries$|INFO entries|WARN entries|missing level
first entry|error message is parsed|HTTP methods|status codes

criteria.description: Edge case parsing (query strings, escaped quotes, special chars)

origin.notes: content variant r2 of regex-extract — same task type, same structure, different log content
